Equally weighting the target prices from the two valuation methods gives a combined target price of $62.3, which implies a 12.7% downside from the current market price. Adding the forward dividend yield gives a total expected return of negative 9.2%.

I think the total expected return isn’t bad enough to warrant a sell rating. Therefore, I’m adopting a hold rating on Northrim BanCorp.

data.title: Sirius XM Makes Sense As A Warren Buffett Investment
data.description:
Sirius XM (NASDAQ:SIRI) is a $9 billion radio and broadcasting corporation, more than 30% owned by Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B), which recently added to its stake. The company has continued to build a unique business while facing competition from streamers such as Spotify. As we'll see throughout this article, the company has an impressive portfolio of assets.

Sirius XM Overview

The company has an incredibly strong position in the market supported by acquisitions and a buildup of assets.

The company has nearly $7 billion in revenue from its core subscription business, which comes from both Sirius XM and Pandora paid subscribers. The company does have the issue that these subscribers tend to be older, and its market penetration is weaker with the younger crowd. However, the company is also building an ad business learning from growing entrants like Netflix (NFLX).

The company has nearly $2 billion in advertising revenue as streamers rediscover what cable companies figured out, people are willing to put up with ads for a cheaper monthly subscription.

Sirius XM Market Share

The company continues to maintain a strong market share, with 21% market share + 2% in its ownership of Pandora.

That means despite competition, the company remains the largest player in the market. The company maintains high satisfaction and price/value surveys. The company's market share is essential to its cash flow, and the company needs to be able to maintain this market share in the face of both established competitors like Spotify (SPOT) and upstarts like Apple Music (AAPL).

The company has continued to maintain high ARPU second to only Netflix. The company has managed to do that with low churn. That combination of factors has supported the company's high gross margins of 60%. The company has 33 million paid subscribers meaning each subscriber is paying almost $200 / year for the company's service.

The company has a strong and established positioning, however, much cheaper competitors such as Spotify could put pressure on the company to maintain its pricing strength.

Sirius XM Transition

The company is working to transition focusing on its strong properties like SoundCloud and Pandora along with ad revenue.

The company is a leading ad-supported streaming provider with 150 million listeners and $1.8 billion in ad revenue. The company's transition involves building additional leading in-car platforms along with continuing to compete in both streaming and younger demos. We think the company can compete in car versus AM / FM but overall music streaming will be difficult.

It's also worth noting that the company has some very wealthy competitors. Specifically Apple and Amazon (AMZN) which both have a reasonable market share and don't need the same profits from their existing business. Their billions to enter a market where profits are unimportant for them could make them difficult companies to compete with.

Sirius XM Forecast

Financially, the company's forecast centers around managing its debt and continuing to drive shareholder returns.

In the immediate term the company is focused on debt reduction, with a hefty net debt position of more than $10 billion. The company's FCF has trickled down while satellite capex has increased, which both present risks as well for the company. The company needs to keep its FCF high enough to drive returns by paying down debt and then driving future shareholder returns.

The company is reducing its share count which it can comfortably afford while continuing to pay strong mid-single digit dividends. Those dividends form a core aspect of the company's shareholder returns, and reward patient investors while the company improves its balance sheet. Overall, the company's improving balance sheet and returns show its shareholder commitment.

There's a reason why Berkshire Hathaway has an impressive market position in the company. The company has a strong moat, strong FCF, and an improving balance sheet.

Thesis Risk

The largest risk to our thesis is that Sirius XM is seeing increased competition, as evidenced in the company's struggling financials. The company's targets are lofty but there's no guarantee that they'll pan out, and if they don't, that could hurt the company's ability to continue its success and continue to generate future shareholder returns.

Conclusion

Berkshire Hathaway has made a hefty investment in Sirius XM and the company is continuing to add to its investment. That's happened as Sirius XM has continued to improve its balance sheet and focus on driving shareholder returns for interested investors. The company has continued to pay a strong dividend and reduce outstanding shares.

The biggest question is can the company transform to a younger audience in a market filled with upstart competitors. We expect the company to continue performing in the vehicle segment; however, the company needs to grow its streaming segment and Pandora while paying down debt. Whether or not the company can do that remains to be seen.

Let us know your thoughts in the comments below.

data.title: Victoria's Secret: No Near-Term Turnaround A Risk To Consensus Estimates
data.description:
We are bearish on Victoria's Secret (NYSE:VSCO). Based on DCF analysis, our target valuation of $15 per share implies a 45% downside from the current level.

We believe that there remains a significant risk to consensus EPS estimates, and we could see a 15% EPS downside in the next few years, given the company's ongoing identity crisis and the growing competition within North America's intimates sector. In addition, we believe that cross-border e-commerce players such as Temu (PDD) and Shein could take incremental shares away from VSCO, given their superior value proposition within a vertical that can be easily commoditized and lacks clear differentiation.

Our thesis on VSCO is as follows:

We believe that VSCO faces a prolonged identity crisis that will take more than a CEO change. VSCO is attempting to remain relevant when its prior core competence in amplifying female sexuality no longer matters in an era that embraces greater inclusivity, physical imperfection, and self-comfort over pure sex appeal in the wake of the MeToo Movement. This structural shift in consumer preference is shaping up in the US and globally. VSCO's current brand transformation from sexy to inclusive diminishes its core competitive advantage, resulting in an identity crisis that will continue to diminish its brand relevance by alienating its core consumers. At the same time, VSCO cannot attract new consumers given the new product mix and marketing message's lack of material differentiation from the emerging competitors.

Competition is intensifying from the more inclusive brands, specialty brands, and, to a certain extent, Chinese e-commerce players. Competition from domestic and international platforms is growing, which will cap VSCO's margin expansion. Emerging brands such as Aerie (AEO), Skim (by Kim Kardashian), and Savage X Fenty (by Rihanna) are gaining better traction with female consumers as these brands' marketing messages better align with the female user base. By offering both breadth and depth in category and size, the emerging brands have gained an edge over VSCO and will likely continue to gain consumer mindshare over time.

Macro remains a headwind. The consumer continues to shift their budget to value-for-money items regarding discretionary spending. VSCO's price point continues to be at the mid to entry-level premium segment, and this positions the product at a disadvantage compared with the subscale emerging brands such as Savage X Fenty, Aerie, and Skim, and digital-only brands such as Temu and Shein that can deliver both comfort and value for money.

The stock has rallied from its August low as the markets are mainly positive on the forward-looking commentary from the company that full-year revenue guidance is improving to down 1% from the low-single-digit range and guiding a respectable $250m - $275m full-year operating profit. This has attracted a combination of long-only buying and hedge fund short-covering, although we believe most of the upside has been driven by short squeeze rather than fundamental.

With the stock trading at 14x forward earnings, similar to its apparel peers, we believe VSCO's current valuation is becoming demanding, given its weak revenue trajectory and uncertainties surrounding its e-commerce and international growth.

We value VSCO at $15 per share based on DCF analysis, assuming a 1% decline in North American store sales in the next five years coupled with a 10% CAGR for international and a 3% CAGR for e-commerce. This translates to a 1.5% five-year revenue CAGR, which, we believe, is reasonable as it will take at least three years for VSCO to turn around its brand and operational strategy if we use Abercrombie (ANF) as a case study.

We applied calculated a cost of equity based on a 3.5% risk-free rate off the US 10-year yield, a beta of 1.5, and an equity risk premium of 6%. Assuming a 60/40 capital structure, we calculate a 10.5% WACC.

We assume a terminal growth of 3%, in line with a normalized inflation rate and an exit multiple of 7x EBITDA, similar to some of the underperforming apparel peers such as Gap (GAP), Urban Outfitters (URBN) and PVH Corp. (PVH).

Our valuation target on VSCO implies 0.6x EV/revenue, which, we believe, is reasonable if we use ANF as a case study. We note that ANF's EV/Revenue multiple collapsed to 0.5x from 1.5x between 2010 and 2016 when the company faced public backlash from its sexually suggestive marketing and exclusive sizing, resulting in sales decline and management change. Since then, ANF has traded below 0.5x EV/revenue between 2016 and late 2019 amid its brand transformation. I believe VSCO should be trading at a similar multiple, given that it faces challenges similar to ANF's.

Brand Identity Is Losing Touch With Consumers

While VSCO dominated the US intimate market in revenue, the business model of selling "sexy" had an apparent flaw that limited its addressable market and flexibility to pivot away from its position.

First, the product was built by men rather than women who understood women's needs. VSCO's focus on "sexy" caters to a specific type of women's apparel with a specific look and appeal. The product line focused heavily on special occasions and specific individuals, rather than daily wearers.

Second, the overall marketing message alienated the mainstream. The marketing targeted a petite female demographic with a skinny physique beyond the mainstream. The singular focus on sexy and skinny immediately alienated mainstream female shoppers with a broad range of body sizes.

While VSCO has undergone a complete brand transformation to address its market share loss by becoming more inclusive, we believe this creates two fundamental challenges when it comes to VSCO's market positioning:

First, this drastic shift away from sexy could alienate VSCO's core consumer base, which may churn and shift their spending to the higher-end brands that still present a more feminine stance, such as La Perla and Agent Provocateur.

Second, the shift towards inclusion diminishes the foundation of what VSCO differentiated from the rest, which was the focus on sexy. When VSCO shifts its market position to the mainstream, its differentiation becomes less clear, while its price point remains higher than its peers'.

The bottom line is that the demand for VSCO products is expected to see continued headwind due to: 1) Unclear market positioning between sexy vs. inclusive with the brand not resonating with the mainstream consumers; 2) The shift towards comfort presents a challenge to VSCO's best-selling and high-margin bra lineup; 3) Panties becoming increasingly commoditized with the emergence of off-price channels, which further exacerbate by the emergence of cross-border Chinese platforms.

According to Google Trends, VSCO's online search only sees spikes during the holiday season, while the rest of the year has been largely stagnant.

Emerging Competition From Temu And Shein Could Result In Further Losses

A common theme amongst the inclusive brands is their appreciation for inclusivity and their focus on product breadth and depth in the plus size segment, which allowed them to take shares from VSCO and is likely to continue to take shares based on proper positioning.

We conducted a detailed analysis of the competitors' websites and mobile apps to gauge their SKU depth and breadth.

On the depth and breadth side, the inclusive brand specializes in expanding the scope of the plus-size segment to cater to a broader array of female consumers. For example, Skims focuses on the premium segment regarding pricing and features, with bra sizes ranging from 30A to 46F and pantie sizes ranging from XXS to 4X. Unlike VSCO, which limits the plus size in assortment and colors, Skim has 12 classic shades for all skin colors, thereby greatly covering a broader consumer base.

On the affordable spectrum, Savage X Fenty attempts to deliver an equal assortment of all-size categories to cover a broader set of consumers than the premium-focused Skim. For example, Savage X Fenty's bra comes in over 50 sizes up to 46DDD, while sleepwear and lingerie sizes range from XS to XL. Most products come in 3 or 4 shades to cater to different skin tones.

While the domestic inclusive brands have gained much attention, we believe that Temu and Shein could be dark horses in the intimate race, given their more significant potential to offer value for money. Temu US offers an extensive array of women's bra sizes, but we focused on the plus size (bust 38 and above). The largest size Temu offers is 46G, which is one cup bigger than Skim's 46F and Savage X Fenty's 46DDD. Below is the table that shows the result item for each size. We placed a cut-off of max 100 for simplicity. Still, we can safely conclude that Temu has a considerable depth in sizes for the bra category, many of which are priced around $5-20/piece, significantly lower than VSCO's $40 average bra price.

Temu offers panties in sizes XS to XXL, and each size produced more than 1,000 items in the resulting search.

Questionable Turnaround Strategy

Some investors are positive about VSCO's expansion into high-growth categories such as sports bras and swimwear and its international growth objective of doubling the revenue in five years.

We do not believe this goal is achievable due to the following:

First, within international retail sales, franchises, and China are the largest revenue contributors to revenue growth. China's intimate markets are more competitive than the US, with a mix of international brands, local brands, and white-label brands from PDD.

Second, VSCO previously ventured into swimwear, only to be pulled from the product line in 2016, as it wanted to focus more on intimates. VSCO recently invested in an emerging swimwear brand, Frankies Bikinis, and plans to introduce its products to stores.

Frankies Bikinis started as a family business focusing on style and inclusivity. Product quality is premium and is priced at the high end. For both the top and the bottom, the total price can be around $200 for a set, which is multiples compared to those sold on Amazon, Abercrombie, Aerie, and Shein, which are priced less than $50 for a set. VSCO's initial entry into swimwear was unsuccessful; we do not see how this time is different.

As for sports bras, it is difficult to differentiate against LULU, NKE, and emerging brands such as Vuori, Girlfriend, and Alo Yoga, given that VSCO historically lacks performance wear expertise.

In conclusion, we are negative on VSCO and believe that the turnaround strategy will likely take longer than expected and that the current consensus is underestimating the challenge of a brand turnaround and the competitive pressure the company is facing. We believe there is significant risk to consensus EPS and that the stock will likely de-rate as the turnaround challenge continues to crystallize.

data.title: Philip Morris Can Slide Even After Strong Q3 2024 Numbers (Rating Downgrade)
data.description:
Since I last wrote about the tobacco giant Philip Morris (NYSE:PM) in August, its price hasn't budged. This isn't a surprise, though. Even at the time, its forward non-GAAP price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio was high, indicating the risk associated with a short-term investment in the stock.

In any case, with its just 37% price increase over the past decade, capital gains aren't PM's highlight. It's the stable and predictable dividends that make it tick. But for investors who aren't looking for just passive income over the long-term the potential returns from PM look unconvincing right now. This is despite the broadly positive outlook for its third quarter (Q3 2024) numbers expected early next week. Here, I discuss what to expect from the upcoming numbers along with reasons for why it's still a good idea to hold-off from buying PM.

What to expect from Q3 2024 results

After a robust performance in the first half of 2024 (H1 2024), the company expects a "strong second-half performance". This remark is likely to be restricted to its earnings expectations, however, because it appears that top line growth can soften.

The company's organic revenue growth was already strong at 10.2% year-on-year (YoY) in H1 2024. By comparison, it expects a smaller 7.5-9% net revenue growth for the full year 2024. Analysts' estimates on Seeking Alpha forecast a 6% YoY growth in Q3 2024, which is in line with the expectation of softer full-year growth.

Next, Philip Morris expects adjusted diluted earnings per share [EPS] to come in the range of USD 1.77-1.82 in the quarter, inclusive of an adverse currency impact. At the midpoint of the guidance range, this implies a healthy sequential increase of ~13% from the USD 1.59 level seen in Q2 2024. Analysts are even more bullish on the earnings figure, expecting it to come in at the top end of the guidance range.

What can hold Philip Morris back

Even with the expected softening in revenue growth, the nice EPS increase can still make for healthy results. But it's unlikely to result in any sustained increase in price. Here are four reasons why:

#1. High market multiples

With the price having stagnated over the past couple of months, its market multiples are exactly where they were when I last checked. According to my estimates, based on the assumption that the adjusted diluted EPS will come in at the midpoint of the company's guidance range, PM's forward non-GAAP ratio is at 18.8x. This compares unfavourably to the stock's five-year average of 16.2x implying a 14% price downside.

There's no solace to be found in other variations of the P/E ratio either (see table below). In fact, it's worth noting that the stock's trailing twelve months [TTM] GAAP P/E is running close to five-year highs right now (see chart below).

#2. Unfavourable placed compared to peers

There might still have been a Buy case for PM, though, if it were the only one to see a plateauing in price while its peers continued to rise. But that's not the case either. Over the past six months, its price increase is second only to Imperial Brands (OTCPK:IMBBY) among the biggest five tobacco stocks by market capitalisation (see chart below). In fact, even over the past month, PM is one of the two tobacco stocks to see a small price rise, with IMBBY being the other one. The remaining three have all seen a softening.

As a result, PM's forward non-GAAP P/E continues to remain significantly higher than that for peers. Altria (MO) comes a distant second with a P/E of just 9.7x, followed by Imperial brands at 9.5x and British American Tobacco (BTI) at 7.5x.

There's some justification for a premium valuation for PM, with its successful pivot to next-generation products. With smoke-free products accounting for 40% of its revenues, it stands out among tobacco companies. But even then, the gap in valuation is glaring. Extending the analysis to the forward P/E for 2025 doesn't help either. With the figure at 17.1x, it's still much higher than that for peers, which are at levels similar to those expected for 2024. It's also higher than the stock's own five-year average.

#3. Stock market gains expected to continue

Even then, the entire tobacco set can stand to gain in the event of market uncertainty, increasing the attractiveness of defensives. But going by both historical trends and projections, this is unlikely to happen.

As the chart from JPMorgan research below shows, the final quarter of the year has historically given the strongest returns. The bank alludes to this as the Santa Claus rally, fuelled by higher consumer spending during the festive season.

Further, Goldman Sachs has actually recently raised its target for S&P 500 (SP500) for this year to 6,000. Admittedly, this is just 2.3% increase from the present levels, but it definitely doesn't suggest any pessimism, which could tilt the balance towards defensives like tobacco stocks.

#4. Relatively smaller dividend yield

With its strong price gains this year, PM's dividend yield is also low compared with peers. It forward dividend yield at 4.5% is much smaller than that for BTI, for example, which has the highest yield of 8.6%. But PM's yield is also much lower than the average forward yield for the big five tobacco stocks at 7%.

What next?

With the stock's forward yield for 2028 at 12.9x, there's still a long-term investment case for it, especially as it builds further on revenues from smoke-free products. The upcoming Q3 2024 results can further underline its progress, besides reflecting healthy EPS increases. In other words, investors looking for predictable dividends over time can still make stand to gain from buying PM.

But the short-to-medium term risk to the stock has become more compelling recently. The stock has made some of the biggest gains among peers in the past half year and is now trading at uncomfortably high market multiples.

If there was a strong case for defensives right now, the downside to the stock could be far more limited, but that's not the case either. In fact, markets are expected to look buoyant in the foreseeable future, while PM's forward P/E continues to look unconvincing into the next year. I'm downgrading it to Hold for now, with a possibility of upgrade if its earnings outlook is revised upwards meaningfully.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Reddit Q3 Preview: Licensing The Next Growth Catalyst After Ads
data.description:
Introduction & Investment Thesis

I last wrote about Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) where I initiated a “buy” rating as the company was seeing steady growth in its advertising revenue and profitability from targeted product investments to drive DAUq (Daily Active Uniques), while building full funnel solutions to yield a higher return on ad spend. At the same time, Reddit had also announced its partnership with OpenAI, where it would be building on OpenAI’s platform of AI models to improve user experience and drive user innovation. Since then, the stock is up over 25%, outperforming the S&P 500 by more than 3x.

The company reported its Q2 FY24 earnings in August, where it beat both its revenue and earnings estimates. This was driven by strong growth in DAUqs from targeted product investments, resulting in advertising revenue growing at a 41% YoY growth rate, with strength across channels, verticals, and geographies. At the same time, its Other Revenue segment, which is primarily driven by Data Licensing, also grew 690% YoY and contributed 10% to Total Revenue.

Reddit is due to report its Q3 FY24 earnings on October 29th, where revenue is expected to grow 44% to approximately $300M, while Adjusted EBITDA is expected to grow on a sequential and year-over-year basis to $50M with a margin of 16%.

In this post, I have discussed some of the key metrics and commentary for investors to pay attention to during the earnings call, while reiterating my “buy” rating as the company takes a two-pronged approach to growing its revenue while maintaining superior operational rigor at the same time.

A quick recap of Reddit’s Q2 FY24 earnings

Reddit reported its Q2 FY24 earnings, where revenue grew at an accelerating pace from the previous quarter at 54% YoY to $281.2M, beating estimates. At the same time, its Adjusted EBITDA grew sequentially and on a year-over-year basis to $39.5M, with a margin expansion of 3300 basis points to 14.0%, given moderating cost growth.

Particularly, when it comes to its revenue trajectory, it is worthwhile pointing out the key factors contributing to its growth rate.

1. DAUq reached an all-time high of 91.2M, growing 51% YoY as a result of continuous product investments to improve the UX to help users explore their interests, discover conversations, and find their communities. For instance, in the pilot phase of their AMA (Ask me Anything) product, hundreds of new AMAs were conducted with high-profile artists and brands, as they made it easier to host, schedule, and participate in AMAs, enabling them to enhance the conversation experience. During Q2, 47% of all screen views occurred on the posts and comment pages in Q2, with the number of comments viewed hitting a new high, growing 10% sequentially from Q1 to Q2, which further showcases the growing engagement of users on the platform. Looking ahead, the management is looking to enhance search and expand subreddit functionalities while simultaneously monetizing their developer platform, which will empower users to create and earn on the platform.

2. International Expansion is a key growth priority for Reddit. During Q2, the company saw international DAUq grow 44% YoY to 45M, with significant growth in countries such as France, India, the U.K., and the Philippines as they expand machine translation to drive user traffic and make Reddit accessible to everyone, regardless of their native language. At the same time, international events such as Eurovision, Euro 2024, and the Olympics also helped boost user growth and engagement on the platform, while the developer platform helped accelerate their verticalization strategy with international communities, particularly in sports where custom features such as scoreboards and polls were used to drive traffic and engagement within the community.

3. Advertising Revenue grew at an accelerated pace of 41% YoY, contributing 90% to Total Revenue. This was driven by increases in impressions delivered as they continued to attract new users and deepen engagement on the platform, with performance advertising driving more than half of their overall growth during the quarter. During the quarter, Reddit saw 50% YoY growth in mid-market and SMB channels as they added new advertisers on the platform while deepening existing relationships, given that their rich product discussions provide a contextually relevant space for advertisers to reach their audience in their purchasing journey. Plus, the company also launched DPA (Dynamic Product Ads), which will be created automatically from advertisers’ product catalogs with targeting options for prospecting and retargeting. So far, DPAs have seen a 1.9x higher return on ad spend when compared to other conversion objective campaigns, which should aid in directing higher advertising spend on the platform in the coming quarters.

4. Other Revenue grew 690% YoY, driven by Data Licensing agreements. In my previous post, I had discussed Reddit’s announcement of its partnership with OpenAI on May 16, where Reddit will leverage OpenAI’s technology to build AI tools and features by licensing its data instead of spending resources to build in-house models, allowing it to monetize its two decades worth of content into a revenue stream while simultaneously accelerating its ad revenue growth by effectively matching user intent with personalized and relevant ads. In Q2, Reddit signed partnerships across the data licensing landscape with OpenAI and Sprinklr, while it continues to explore new agreements with collaborative partners in the ecosystem, ranging from marketing intelligence companies to enterprise technology companies.

Things to watch out for in the upcoming Q3 FY24 earnings call

Reddit is due to report its Q3 FY24 earnings on October 29th, where it is expected to generate revenue in the range of $290-$310M, which would represent over 44% YoY growth rate. In the meantime, the company has also guided for Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $40-$60M, which would represent a 16% margin, a 200 basis point increase sequentially, and over 1900 basis points on a year-over-year basis.

While beating the above estimates will be a positive sign, investors should be paying attention to trends in the metrics below to assess its long-term investment thesis moving forward.

  1. DAUqs: With the rate of revenue growth expected to slow down on a sequential basis, we can expect the same for DAUqs. So far, US DAUqs represent 50% of all DAUqs and are growing at a faster rate than international DAUqs. Given the company’s strategic focus on expanding internationally with its immersive machine translation, while driving targeted product investments to deepen engagement by helping users discover conversations, it will be interesting to see the distribution and trend of US to international DAUqs in the coming quarters.

  2. Advertising Revenue: Although Reddit has close to equal distribution of DAUqs between its US and international regions, its US revenue far outpaces its international revenue by more than 4x. So far, the company has made sizable progress in building its full funnel advertising solutions, resulting in a doubling of click volume for the third consecutive quarter as it added new advertisers while deepening relations with existing ones. Moving forward, it will be important to watch for management commentary on how it sees advertiser spend trends after the US elections are over, as well as its progress in activating new advertisers in its international markets. It is also important to note that Reddit faces competition from Big Tech companies such as Google (NASDAQ:GOOG), Meta (NASDAQ:META) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), however, given its high user intent-driven content, advertisers may be inclined to drive spending for precision targeting and a higher return on ad spend.

  3. Other Revenue: This is primarily driven by Data Licensing Revenue and as Reddit enters into new partnerships across the data licensing landscape, we should expect the segment to grow at a substantially faster rate than Advertising Revenue and contribute a larger share to Total Revenue.

  4. Adjusted EBITDA: I believe one of the reasons behind the stock’s outperformance since its IPO has been related to its demonstration of operational rigor, where it has been steadily growing its Adjusted EBITDA and margin throughout FY24 as a result of streamlining operating costs, which has been growing at a much slower pace compared to overall revenue growth, enabling the company to unlock operating leverage. However, if revenue growth is to moderate from current levels into next year and beyond as per consensus estimates in the low twenties range, investors need to pay attention to the rate of growth of non-GAAP operating expenses moving forward.

Revisiting my valuation

Looking forward, I will take the consensus estimates for revenue growth into account (similar to my previous post), which sees Reddit growing in the low twenties range over the next two years after FY24 to generate approximately $1.6B in revenue. This will be driven by growth in its advertising and data licensing revenue. On the advertising front, I believe that the company’s product investments will continue to attract and engage DAUqs on the platform, which lays the foundation for building effective advertising solutions to drive higher return on ad spend for advertisers on the platform, creating a flywheel effect. On the data licensing front, we can expect that its existing partnerships with OpenAI and Sprinklr, along with potential new partners, will pave the way for Other Revenue to contribute to a larger share of Total Revenue.

Given that the company has not provided a long-term operating model for Adjusted EBITDA margin, I will base my valuation of Reddit on revenue growth projections over the next 3 years. Therefore, assuming that Reddit generates approximately $1.6B by FY26, that would be equivalent to a present value of $1.38B when discounted 8%.

Taking the S&P 500 as a proxy, where its companies grow their revenues at an average rate of 4.8% with a price-to-sales ratio of 2.19, that would mean that Reddit should be trading at roughly 4.5 times the multiple given the growth rate of its revenue during this period of time. Please note that these assumptions have not changed from my previous post. This will translate to a P/S ratio of 10, or a price target of $84.4, which represents an upside of 11% from its current levels.

My final verdict and conclusions

Since the time of my last writing, the magnitude of the upside has shrunk from 33% to 11%. However, I will maintain my “buy” rating at its current levels, as the company continues to execute well in a highly competitive environment while keeping its capital expenditures at a minimal rate of 0.4% of revenue.

The way I see it, Reddit is taking a two-pronged approach to growing its revenue in the coming years. On one hand, its advertising revenue should continue to grow as it builds its full-funnel solutions and takes advantage of enhanced AI capabilities to unlock user insights to drive higher return on ad spend for its advertisers, resulting in higher advertiser spend directed on the platform. Plus, its international markets have plenty of whitespace to drive user monetization in the coming years. On the other hand, its data licensing revenue should also continue to scale with OpenAI and Sprinklr while it explores new data licensing agreements with collaborative partners. In all of this, the company is managing to keep its costs under control, with Adjusted EBITDA steadily growing with expanding margins.

While the stock may be subject to short-term volatility, given that its magnitude of upside has shrunk, I believe that directionally the company is on the right track. Although advertising demand remains an area of risk for next year, I believe that should not deter its long-term growth story as it drives targeted investments and operational discipline, making it a "buy."

data.title: Kratos: Software-Driven, And Aligned With The DoD
data.description:
In my last thesis on L3Harris Technologies (LHX), I focused on how it is aligned with the DoD's (Department of Defense) call for the integration of commercial technologies into defense applications.

This time, the aim is to highlight how Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (NASDAQ:KTOS) has a similar approach which puts it in a better position to win contracts. Also, while there are risks associated with being a relatively smaller player in a sector consisting of big defense contractors, the company's software-driven approach puts it in a better position to scale in a profitable way.

Trading around $24.85 at the time of writing, it has gained about 45% during the last year but remains below its February 2021 high of $32.5.

I start by elaborating how selling cutting-edge technologies developed for the commercial world to the military ensures its alignment with the DoD’s call for the defense sector to benefit from more affordable innovation.

Aligned with the DoD's Commercial Mandate

First, the DoD released a report this year highlighting the problem of isolation in the industry whereby defense contractors have grown increasingly isolated from the commercial sector throughout the years thereby hindering innovation. The issue has been exacerbated by consolidation resulting in the number of players being reduced from above 50 to less than 10 dominant ones leading not only to reduced competition as these have been largely profit-driven but also to isolation from the broader commercial economy. This also limits investments in defense-related technologies and access to emerging technologies, including cybersecurity and AI.

Furthermore, the DoD also stressed the need for more collaboration with commercial entities as a way to increase the level of competition within the Defense Industrial Base (DIB) or the industrial military complex of the United States. Thus, for those competing for contracts as part of the DoD's planned $841.4 billion budget this year, they may be in a better position to get selected if they already have a commercial presence, either individually or through partnerships, like L3Harris with Palantir (PLTR) for advanced AI and data analytics.

As for Kratos, it accelerates breakthroughs to national security through proven commercial technology approaches. To this end, it specializes in technologies like UAS (unmanned aerial systems), satellite communications, cybersecurity, and AI or technologies which by their very nature are dual-use, meaning they can be deployed in both commercial and defense applications.

Moreover, its area of expertise also includes affordable technology solutions for national security. Thus, in contrast to larger defense contractors whose products may be more costly and take time to develop, Kratos approach seems more aligned with the DoD's not only in terms of delivery time but also for cost-effective defense systems. This resonates well in a period of budgetary constraints.

On the other hand, more affordable solutions generally mean lower margins.

Lower Margins but Improving Rapidly

In this respect, its strategy to focus on emerging technologies and achieve a “first to market” position means investing in R&D and has translated into profit margins lagging the median for the defense sector as shown below. Digging deeper, after years of consolidation, the aerospace and defense industry comprises large players like Lockheed Martin (LMT) or Northrop Grumman (NOC). However, as the business has been scaling, progress has been made, namely for EBITDA and levered FCF margins which now respectively exceed their five-year averages.

Looking further, R&D as a percentage of revenues decreased by 0.5% to 3.4% in the second quarter of 2024 (Q2) compared to the same period last year with SG&A also decreasing by 0.2% to 18.2%. This decrease is partly explained by lower research expenses incurred for the space and satellite communications business (which forms part of the Kratos Government Solutions segment alongside Turbine Technologies), offsetting the increase for the Unmanned Systems segment.

Moreover, EBITDA has grown at 93% YoY which exceeds the sector median by a whopping 1,364% which means it deserves to be valued better. However, its forward EV/EBITDA of 35.55x is overpriced by 191.5% as shown below.

Deserves Better as a Software-Driven Defense Play

However, basing its value on a comparison with peers would underestimate its potential in IT software. Looking across the economy, in the same way like companies like Arista Networks (ANET) have revolutionized the IT networking world with affordable and more easily deployable cloud solutions, Kratos has collaborated with ALL.SPACE, a manufacturer of satellite terminals, to develop a software-defined satellite ground systems.

This aims is to create standardized and flexible terminals that are cheaper compared to more hardware-based systems. Along the same lines, there is the partnership with Radisys to develop cloud native 5G-NTN (non-terrestrial network) solutions for satellite connectivity. For investors, a cloud native solution is more advanced than conventional cloud computing since it is faster to deploy. Interestingly, these solutions will be available through Kratos OpenSpace Platform, in the same way as Arista's AI networking platform.

Therefore, through its software-defined and cloud-native technologies, Kratos is more than an Industrials sector play developing hardware for satellite communications and drones. As such, its deserves a better value than what can be identified through a sector-wise comparison.

For this purpose, as shown below, its forward EV/EBITDA ratio is below Arista's 38.8x, by 9.6%. Therefore, I have a target of $27.23 based on the current share price of $24.85. As a side note, EV/EBITDA is an appropriate metric because it offers a comprehensive view of a company's total value by accounting for both equity and debt but without cash. Thus, it provides a better idea of core operating performance for comparison across different sectors.

However, this bullish position is based on the company continuing to grow EBITDA at a fast pace, but it faces competition from larger contractors possessing more financial resources, which can lead to pricing pressures and reduced profits.

Expect Volatility Risks

First, it lacks the economies of scale of its larger peers signifying less ability to spread out fixed costs over larger revenue bases, resulting in lower gross margins. Thus, cost of sales increased by $29.9 million, 15.5% YoY to $222.9 million in Q2 mostly due to the Unmanned Systems segment. This resulted in lower margins for the product business but due to revenues advancing at a rapid pace and strength on the services side, gross margins improved by 21% YoY.

Second, its cash per share is lower as unlike larger peers generating significant cash flow from operations from selling expensive systems such as fighter jets, combat vessels or missile defense systems, it generates relatively less through its lower-cost solutions.

Thus, for those looking to buy stocks which generate superior, consistent and predictable cash flow, Kratos which mostly invests in emerging technologies where there are higher risks of delivering uneven returns may be unsuitable. Noteworthily, in case it is not able to generate enough cash, this may lead the company to borrow more money or sell equities as has been the case this year as shown below, thereby diluting the EPS.

Moreover, revenues generated from U.S. Federal Government during Q2 was approximately 65% of overall sales and included contracts with the DoD, non-DoD federal government agencies and FMS (Foreign Military Sales) contracts. These include Israel's Iron Dome missile defense system and Kratos has deep relationships with the Israeli Aerospace Industries. This explains why its share price has appreciated more than its larger peers during last month, a period which included Iran firing at least 180 missiles at the Jewish state.

Therefore, while the share price could increase further in case of an escalation with Iran, expect volatility in case of news update hinting at de-escalation or a diplomatic solution to the Gaza conflict. It could also face uncertainty due to potential fluctuations in defense spending after the November elections.

This is the reason the 9.6% targeted upside remains reasonable, which I justify further based on the following five reasons.

Five Reasons to Justify the Upside

First, it has been winning more contracts for its drones in the Middle East providing it with new markets to expand, together with growth potential in satellite business. Also, its engine and propulsion system business (Kratos Turbine Technologies) is expected contribute to future growth as part of a record backlog. In this respect as summarized in the table below, both the market for space-based networks and unmanned systems is expected to grow at double digits in the next five years and beyond.

Second, looking into profitability, by converting more functions to become software-driven compared to more cumbersome hardware-based systems, Kratos can scale products rapidly while incurring relatively lower incremental expenses, thereby increasing margins. To this end analysts expect the company to expand EPS by 25% in 2024 and sustain the increase during subsequent years as illustrated below.

This increase in profitability is also due to government contracts initially at an early-stage development phases maturing and transitioning to full scale production. One example is the XQ-Valkyrie program which is gaining traction both in the U.S and internationally as part of the global shift towards unmanned drones with more autonomy.

Third, instead of competing head-on with major defense contractors, I have noted that the company has opted for a partnership approach namely by supporting Northrop Grumman, Lockheed Martin, and Raytheon (RTX) through various collaborative efforts. One of them is C5ISR (Command, Control, Computers, Communications, Cyber, Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance) used mostly to enhance decision-making during military operations. This collaboration helped the company generate a revenue growth of nearly 17% YoY in Q2.

Fourth, the balance sheet held $308 million of debt versus $303 million of cash at the end of Q2 and with the Fed on a path to easing monetary policy, it can borrow more at cheaper rates for refinancing purposes.

Equally important, it has a lower debt-to-equity compared to its giant peers as shown below while its capital expenditures remaining relatively constant or between $44 million to $45 million during the last three years, in contrast to its peers whose expenses have gradually increased during this period. Looking for an explanation as to why it has managed to stabilize investments while delivering growth, Kratos leverages the innovative nature and rapid go-to-market potential of startups such as Atreus Technologies for systems in the defense and aerospace sector.

This is just one example and highlights Kratos' commitment to collaborating with smaller, innovative firms which allow it to reduce upfront cash investments while still being competitive on the technological front. Finally, this type of partnership aligns it with one of the DoD's aims of encouraging defense contractors to collaborate with commercial entities and also increases it chances of winning contracts. Also, by sharing costs, it means it does not have to sacrifice profitability while delivering innovative solutions to the American defense industry in an affordable way.
